note: Do not tune prompts, expected outcomes, or thresholds from GAMMA diagnostic failures.
arm_prompts: Several services show resource pressure. Identify abnormal CPU and memory usage across the application.
Find which services are constrained by processor or memory saturation during this window.
Investigate service-level resource saturation and show the CPU and memory evidence.

control_cases.prompt: Review current service health and show useful evidence without assuming a fault.
Check whether the services look healthy in this window; do not invent an incident.
Summarize the available service-health evidence and only flag a cause if the data supports one.
Inspect this quiet window for abnormal resource behavior without presuming degradation.
Show a neutral service-health view for this period and avoid naming an unsupported cause.
Are the application and resources behaving normally here? Base any conclusion on visible evidence.
Review this baseline window for service health without assuming network trouble.
Present useful baseline telemetry and abstain from causal claims unless they are supported.
Check the pre-incident service state and report only abnormalities that are actually visible.
Build a conservative health view for this stable interval with no assumed bottleneck.
Requests slowed during this window. Show the symptom, but do not assert a resource cause without evidence.
Response time degraded. Visualize the application symptom and leave the cause unresolved if resources are absent.
Investigate slower requests using the evidence available here; do not infer an unseen infrastructure fault.
Show where application latency changed and explicitly avoid guessing why when causal telemetry is missing.
Users observed a slowdown. Surface request timing and traffic evidence without blaming memory or CPU.
Confirm whether response latency moved in this interval, and abstain from a resource diagnosis.
Requests took longer. Show the application-level evidence without assuming a network bottleneck.
Examine the reported delay using only observable request signals and make no unsupported causal claim.
Chart the service slowdown, but keep infrastructure causes open because resource evidence is unavailable.
Determine whether request behavior changed and stop short of naming a bottleneck without supporting metrics.
